concepts.name: Circular Queue
concepts.short_definition: A queue implementation that uses a fixed-size array and wraps around to reuse space.

concepts.name: Queue
concepts.short_definition: A linear data structure that follows the First-In, First-Out principle.

concepts.name: Array
concepts.short_definition: A contiguous block of memory storing elements of the same data type.

concepts.name: Linked List
concepts.short_definition: A linear data structure where elements are linked using pointers.

concepts.name: Node
concepts.short_definition: A basic building block of linked data structures, containing data and a reference.

concepts.name: Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A variable that stores the memory address of another variable.

concepts.name: Data Structure
concepts.short_definition: A way of organizing and storing data for efficient access and modification.

concepts.name: Memory Management
concepts.short_definition: The process of allocating and deallocating memory for programs.

concepts.name: Enqueue
concepts.short_definition: The operation of adding an element to the rear of a queue.

concepts.name: Dequeue
concepts.short_definition: The operation of removing an element from the front of a queue.

concepts.name: Front Pointer
concepts.short_definition: An index or pointer to the first element in a queue.

concepts.name: Rear Pointer
concepts.short_definition: An index or pointer to the last element in a queue.

concepts.name: Fixed Size
concepts.short_definition: A data structure with a predetermined maximum capacity.

concepts.name: Wrap Around
concepts.short_definition: The behavior of indices returning to the beginning of an array after reaching the end.

concepts.name: Modulo Operator
concepts.short_definition: An arithmetic operator that returns the remainder of a division.

concepts.name: Arithmetic Operations
concepts.short_definition: Basic mathematical operations like addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division.

concepts.name: Full Queue
concepts.short_definition: The state where a queue cannot accept new elements due to reaching its capacity.

concepts.name: Empty Queue
concepts.short_definition: The state where a queue contains no elements.

concepts.name: Capacity
concepts.short_definition: The maximum number of elements a data structure can hold.

concepts.name: Index
concepts.short_definition: A numerical identifier for the location of an element within an array.

concepts.name: Overwriting
concepts.short_definition: Replacing existing data with new data at the same location.

concepts.name: Space Efficiency
concepts.short_definition: How effectively a data structure utilizes memory.

concepts.name: Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the time required to execute an algorithm as a function of input size.

concepts.name: Algorithm Analysis
concepts.short_definition: The process of evaluating the efficiency of algorithms.

concepts.name: Constant Time
concepts.short_definition: An operation that takes the same amount of time regardless of input size.

concepts.name: Linear Time
concepts.short_definition: An operation whose execution time grows linearly with the input size.

concepts.name: Array-based Implementation
concepts.short_definition: A data structure implemented using an array.

concepts.name: Front Index
concepts.short_definition: The index pointing to the front element of the circular queue.

concepts.name: Rear Index
concepts.short_definition: The index pointing to the position where the next element will be added.

concepts.name: Initialization
concepts.short_definition: The process of setting up a data structure to its initial state.

concepts.name: Element
concepts.short_definition: A single piece of data stored within a data structure.

concepts.name: Queue Operations
concepts.short_definition: The standard set of actions that can be performed on a queue.

concepts.name: Circular Buffer
concepts.short_definition: A data structure that uses a fixed-size buffer as if it were connected end-to-end.

concepts.name: Data Integrity
concepts.short_definition: Ensuring that data is accurate, consistent, and reliable.

concepts.name: Edge Cases
concepts.short_definition: Unusual or extreme situations that a program must handle correctly.

concepts.name: Implementation Details
concepts.short_definition: Specific choices made when building a data structure.

concepts.name: Performance
concepts.short_definition: The speed and efficiency of a data structure's operations.

concepts.name: Underlying Data Structure
concepts.short_definition: The primary data structure on which another is built.

concepts.name: Dynamic Resizing
concepts.short_definition: The ability of a data structure to automatically adjust its size.
